prompt: Return the top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023, where average price is computed as total revenue divided by total units sold, along with total units sold per brand
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    pr.brand,
    ROUND(SUM(sl.Total_Sale_Value) / SUM(sl.Qty_Sold), 2) AS realized_avg_selling_price,
    SUM(sl.Qty_Sold) AS total_units_sold
FROM
    sales sl
JOIN
    products pr ON sl.SKU_Coded = pr.SKU_Coded
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sl.Sold_Date) = 2023
GROUP BY
    pr.brand
ORDER BY
    realized_avg_selling_price DESC
LIMIT 3
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 3 rows with columns : brand, realized_avg_selling_price, total_units_sold
Analyzer_1_message: The top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023 are CrunchTime ($18.22, 24,817 units sold), GlowUp ($17.93, 38,045 units sold), and CleanHome ($17.11, 57,576 units sold). The data shows CrunchTime had the highest average price despite selling the fewest units, while CleanHome sold the most units at a lower average price.

prompt: Show promo revenue share by product category for 2023 as a bar chart, expressing the share as a percentage of total revenue
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL

WITH promo_sales AS (
  SELECT
    p.category,
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.On_Promo = 1 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS promo_revenue,
    SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue
  FROM
    sales s
  JOIN
    products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded
  WHERE
    YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2023
  GROUP BY
    p.category
)
SELECT
  category,
  ROUND((promo_revenue / total_revenue) * 100, 2) AS promo_revenue_share_pct,
  promo_revenue,
  total_revenue
FROM
  promo_sales
ORDER BY
  promo_revenue_share_pct DESC

'''

The resulting DataFrame has 6 rows with columns : category, promo_revenue_share_pct, promo_revenue, total_revenue
